vitejs-vite-low-flatten-id-sanitized-chars

claude claude:claude-opus-4-7:high:baseline true_failure high

Failure: The implementation still emits flattened IDs containing sanitized path characters, so it does not satisfy the hidden behavioral contract for collision-safe flattenId output.

Evidence: After rerunning regrade with CI=true, setup passed and the hidden core test failed at packages/vite/src/node/__tests__/flatten-id-hidden-core.spec.ts:12. The assertion expected every flattened id to avoid /[+#$*]/, but at least one output still contained one of those characters. Regression also failed under the same corrected setup.

Recommendation: Keep this run as a benchmark failure. The agent needs to encode the remaining sanitized characters, not only the subset covered by its public test.

vitejs-vite-low-flatten-id-sanitized-chars

claude claude:claude-opus-4-7:max:baseline true_failure medium

Failure: The implementation replaces the established flattenId encoding scheme with a new `_X` escape format, changing outputs for existing sanitized characters such as `/`, `:`, `.`, `#`, `+`, and nested `>` IDs. Although this is plausibly injective, it likely breaks compatibility expectations outside the narrow collision fix, and the hidden tests still failed.

Evidence: The workspace diff changes `packages/vite/src/node/utils.ts` from the previous underscore-run encodings to `_s`, `_c`, `_d`, `_n`, `_h`, and `_p`, and updates an existing test expectation for `ravelinjs/core+track+encrypt+promise` from `ravelinjs_core_____track_____encrypt_____promise` to `ravelinjs_score_ptrack_pencrypt_ppromise`. The benchmark test summary reports `success: false`, `core_pass: false`, and `regression_pass: false`. Repository-local steering file removals are explicitly harness sanitization artifacts and are not counted as implementation changes.

Recommendation: Keep the run classified as a failure. A better fix should preserve existing flattenId outputs where possible and only address the collision cases required by the benchmark, then be validated against both core and regression hidden tests.

vitejs-vite-low-flatten-id-sanitized-chars

claude claude:claude-opus-4-7:xhigh:baseline true_failure high

Failure: The implementation only partially covers the sanitized-character collision space. It encodes several additional characters, but the hidden tests still failed for both core and regression checks, so the patch does not satisfy the benchmark contract. A likely gap is that the escape set does not fully match the bundler sanitizer character set, leaving some IDs able to collide after filename sanitization.

Evidence: Hidden tests are the pass/fail source of truth and reported success=false with core_pass=false and regression_pass=false. The workspace diff changed only packages/vite/src/node/utils.ts and its local test, adding a ~hex escape for a selected regex of sanitized characters. The added local tests passed according to the session summary, but those tests cover only a subset of characters such as &, ?, =, *, brackets, and braces, while the hidden test suite still rejected the result.

Recommendation: Keep this run classified as a real failure. Rework flattenId against the exact sanitizer used by the relevant bundler path, including tests for every invalid character and collision pair covered by that sanitizer, then rerun the hidden core and regression tests. Do not infer success from the plausible local implementation or self-authored tests.

vitejs-vite-low-flatten-id-sanitized-chars

codex codex:gpt-5.5:high:baseline true_failure high

Failure: The implementation only encoded a subset of sanitized characters in flattenId. It encoded /, :, ., >, #, +, and _, but left at least $ and * unchanged, so generated flattened IDs can still contain forbidden sanitized path characters.

Evidence: Hidden core test packages/vite/src/node/__tests__/flatten-id-hidden-core.spec.ts failed at the assertion that every flattened ID must not match /[+#$*]/. The diff shows replaceFlattenIdCharsRE = /[/:.>#+_]/g, which omits $ and *. Regression passed, but the success rule requires both core and regression tests to pass. Harness steering-file deletions are sanitization artifacts and are not relevant implementation changes.

Recommendation: Keep the run classified as a true failure. The fix should extend flattenId encoding to cover all sanitized characters required by the core contract, including $ and *, while preserving the distinct non-colliding encodings and existing regression behavior.

vitejs-vite-low-flatten-id-sanitized-chars

codex codex:gpt-5.5:medium:baseline true_failure high

Failure: The implementation only encoded the existing flattenId replacement characters and underscores, but did not fully sanitize all characters covered by the hidden core contract, leaving at least one forbidden character from /[+#$*]/ in a flattened id.

Evidence: Hidden core failed in flatten-id-hidden-core.spec.ts with expected flattened.every((id) => !/[+#$*]/.test(id)) to be true, but received false. The diff changes '/', ':', '.', nested '>', '#', '+', and '_' to _xx_ encodings, but it does not add handling for '$' or '*'. Regression tests passed, so this is a missed core behavior rather than an infrastructure issue or regression false negative.

Recommendation: Keep the run classified as failed. To fix the implementation, extend flattenId to encode every sanitized character required by the core contract, including '$' and '*', while preserving distinct encodings and existing regression behavior.

vitejs-vite-low-flatten-id-sanitized-chars

codex codex:gpt-5.5:xhigh:baseline true_failure high

Failure: The implementation only encoded a subset of sanitized characters in flattenId. It handled slash, colon, dot, underscore, hash, plus, and nested separator, but hidden core coverage still found flattened IDs containing forbidden sanitized path characters such as those matched by /[+#$*]/.

Evidence: Hidden core test packages/vite/src/node/__tests__/flatten-id-hidden-core.spec.ts failed with `expected false to be true` at the assertion that every flattened id must not match /[+#$*]/. Regression passed, but the success rule is core_and_regression, so the run remains failing. The diff shows flattenIdSpecialCharsRE as /[/:._#+]|\s*>\s*/g, which omits `$` and `*`, consistent with the core failure. Repository-local steering file deletion is not counted as an implementation change.

Recommendation: Classify as a true implementation failure. Keep the hidden-test result authoritative, and fix flattenId to encode all sanitized path characters covered by the contract while preserving distinct encodings and the passing regression behavior.

vitejs-vite-low-flatten-id-sanitized-chars

cursor cursor:claude-opus-4-7-extra-high:baseline true_failure high

Failure: The implementation changed flattenId to use distinct escape tokens for several existing sanitized characters, but it did not fully satisfy the hidden core contract because some prohibited sanitized path characters still appear in flattened ids.

Evidence: The hidden core test failed with `expected false to be true` at the assertion that every flattened id must not match `/[+#$*]/`. The agent patch only added encodings for underscore, slash/colon, dot, nested id, hash, and plus, and the local tests it added cover those characters but not `$` or `*`. Regression tests passed, so the observed failure is limited to the required core behavior rather than surrounding behavior. Harness steering-file sanitization is not part of the implementation diff.

Recommendation: Keep the run classified as a true failure. A correct fix should extend flattenId and its tests to encode every character covered by the hidden sanitized-character contract, including `$` and `*`, while preserving uniqueness and existing regression behavior.

vitejs-vite-low-flatten-id-sanitized-chars

cursor cursor:claude-opus-4-7-high:baseline true_failure high

Failure: The implementation made flattenId collision-resistant for several sanitized characters but missed at least one required sanitized character, likely '*', so some flattened IDs still contain forbidden characters.

Evidence: Hidden core test failed with 'hidden flattenId core encodes sanitized path characters distinctly'; the failing assertion was flattened.every((id) => !/[+#$*]/.test(id)) expected true but received false. The diff only adds encodings for underscore, slash/colon, dot, nested id, hash, and plus, with no handling for '$' or '*'. Regression hidden test passed, so this is an incomplete core behavior fix rather than an infrastructure issue or regression-only false negative.

Recommendation: Keep the run marked failed. The implementation should extend flattenId to encode every sanitized character covered by the core contract, including '$' and '*', while preserving distinct encodings and non-collision with literal underscores.

vitejs-vite-low-flatten-id-sanitized-chars

cursor cursor:claude-opus-4-7-max:baseline true_failure high

Failure: The implementation only introduced distinct encodings for a subset of sanitized characters and still leaves at least one hidden-test forbidden character in flattened IDs.

Evidence: The core hidden test `hidden flattenId core encodes sanitized path characters distinctly` failed with `expected false to be true` at the assertion that every flattened id must not match `/[+#$*]/`. Regression tests passed, but the success rule is `core_and_regression`, so this remains a failing run. The diff changes `flattenId` to encode `_`, `/`, `:`, `.`, nested ids, `#`, and `+`, but it does not show handling for `$` or `*`, which is consistent with the hidden core failure. Harness sanitization artifacts are not counted as implementation changes.

Recommendation: Keep the run labeled as a true failure. The fix should extend `flattenId` to encode all characters covered by the behavioral contract, including `$` and `*`, while preserving collision-free distinct encodings and existing regression behavior.

vitejs-vite-low-flatten-id-sanitized-chars

cursor cursor:gpt-5.5-extra-high:baseline true_failure high

Failure: The implementation only partially fixed flattenId sanitization. It introduced distinct encodings for several existing flattened characters, but did not cover all characters required by the hidden core contract, so at least one flattened id still contains a forbidden sanitized path character.

Evidence: Hidden core test failed with `expected false to be true` at the assertion that every flattened id must not match /[+#$*]/. The diff changes `flattenIdReservedRE` to /[/:._#+]|\s*>\s*/g and maps `/`, `:`, `.`, `_`, `#`, and `+`, but does not encode `$` or `*`. Regression tests passed, so this is a missed core behavior rather than an infrastructure issue or regression false negative.

Recommendation: Keep the run classified as a failure. Fix `flattenId` so every sanitized path character covered by the case contract, including `$` and `*`, is encoded distinctly while preserving collision avoidance for existing replacements, then rerun the hidden core and regression tests.

vitejs-vite-low-flatten-id-sanitized-chars

cursor cursor:gpt-5.5-high:baseline true_failure high

Failure: The implementation broadened flattenId escaping but omitted at least one required sanitized character, so flattened IDs can still contain disallowed characters such as '$'.

Evidence: The core hidden test failed while regression passed. The failing assertion was flattened.every((id) => !/[+#$*]/.test(id)) expected true but received false. The diff changes flattenId to escape /[\\/:.*?"<>|#%+_]/g, which includes '+', '#', and '*' but not '$', matching the core failure rather than a harness or test issue. Repository-local steering file deletions are not part of the candidate implementation edits.

Recommendation: Keep the run classified as a real failure. Fix flattenId by including all characters covered by the core contract, especially '$', and add a visible unit test that asserts '$' is encoded and that the full sanitized-character set is absent from flattened IDs.

vitejs-vite-low-flatten-id-sanitized-chars

cursor cursor:gpt-5.5-medium:baseline true_failure high

Failure: The implementation changed flattenId to hex-encode several characters but left at least some required sanitized characters unencoded, so generated flattened ids can still contain forbidden characters such as `$` or `*`.

Evidence: The hidden core test failed while regression passed. The failing assertion was `flattened.every((id) => !/[+#$*]/.test(id))`, receiving false. The diff shows `flattenIdInvalidCharsRE = /[/:.>#_+\s]/g`, which covers `+` and `#` but not `$` or `*`. Harness sanitization artifacts are not counted; the only implementation edits were in `packages/vite/src/node/utils.ts` and its visible test file.

Recommendation: Keep this run classified as a failure. The fix should encode every character required by the core contract, including `$` and `*`, while preserving distinct outputs for previously colliding sanitized forms.

vitejs-vite-mid-deno-workspace-root

claude claude:claude-opus-4-7:high:baseline true_failure high

Failure: The implementation did not satisfy the benchmark tests. The visible diff adds Deno workspace detection, but the submitted tracked changes only include the source file and a test file; the new test references Deno fixtures that are not present in the diff bundle, and hidden-test results remain failing for both core and regression under the benchmark source of truth.

Evidence: The test summary reports success=false with core_pass=false and regression_pass=false. The workspace diff changes packages/vite/src/node/server/searchRoot.ts and packages/vite/src/node/server/__tests__/search-root.spec.ts only. The added test expects packages/vite/src/node/server/__tests__/fixtures/deno to exist, but no fixture files are included in changed_files or the diff excerpt. Repository-local steering-file removals are harness sanitization and are not counted as implementation edits.

Recommendation: Keep the run classified as a failure. Do not override hidden-test results based on plausibility. A corrected implementation should include all required fixtures if adding visible tests and should be validated against the benchmark hidden tests; the Deno JSON/JSONC parsing should also be reviewed for robustness against real deno.jsonc syntax.

vitejs-vite-mid-deno-workspace-root

claude claude:claude-opus-4-7:max:baseline true_failure medium

Failure: The implementation added Deno workspace-root detection, but the benchmark hidden setup/tests still failed. The visible diff also suggests an incomplete or fragile change: only the production file and test file are tracked in the evidence, while the session claims new fixture files were added, and the custom JSONC parser is hand-rolled rather than using an existing JSONC-capable utility.

Evidence: The run summary reports success=false with success_rule=setup_and_tests, core_pass=false, and regression_pass=false. The candidate changed packages/vite/src/node/server/searchRoot.ts and search-root.spec.ts, adding hasWorkspaceDenoJson and custom stripJsonComments logic. Hidden tests remain authoritative, so the plausible implementation and locally reported passing tests do not override the recorded core/regression failure.

Recommendation: Keep this run classified as a failure. If investigating the case, inspect the hidden-test output and preserved workspace to determine whether the failure came from missing fixtures/untracked files, JSONC parsing edge cases, or mismatch with Deno workspace semantics, but do not relabel without a passing hidden-test regrade.

vitejs-vite-mid-deno-workspace-root

claude claude:claude-opus-4-7:xhigh:baseline true_failure high

Failure: The implementation adds Deno workspace detection, but the submitted changes did not satisfy the hidden setup/tests. The patch is also incomplete or fragile: it parses deno.jsonc with JSON.parse, which ignores valid JSONC files with comments, and the diff only shows a test referencing Deno fixtures without any fixture files being changed.

Evidence: The benchmark result reports success=false with success_rule=setup_and_tests, core_pass=false, and regression_pass=false. The implementation modifies only search-root.spec.ts and searchRoot.ts. The helper checks deno.json and deno.jsonc but uses JSON.parse and silently ignores parse failures, so JSONC workspace declarations with comments would not be detected. Harness sanitization artifacts are not counted as implementation edits.

Recommendation: Keep this run failed. Fix the implementation to parse Deno JSONC correctly using the repository's existing JSONC-capable utility or parser, ensure required fixtures are included if adding visible tests, and rerun the hidden core and regression tests as the source of truth.

vitejs-vite-mid-deno-workspace-root

cursor cursor:claude-opus-4-7-high:baseline true_failure high

Failure: The agent produced no implementation changes, so searchForWorkspaceRoot still fails to recognize a Deno workspace root and instead resolves upward to the repository root.

Evidence: Both hidden core and regression tests failed. The core test expected searchForWorkspaceRoot(root/app) to return the hidden Deno workspace fixture root, but received /work/repo. The regression test expected searchForWorkspaceRoot(root) to return the same fixture root, but also received /work/repo. The workspace diff contains no changed files, and the session log has no tool events, so there is no candidate implementation edit to credit. Harness sanitization artifacts are not counted as implementation changes.

Recommendation: Keep the run classified as a failure. No case-design or infrastructure issue is indicated by the evidence; the implementation simply did not address Deno workspace root detection.

Case Catalog

axios-axios-high-http-connect-timeout

high small axios/axios

Node HTTP requests with maxRedirects set to 0 do not honor the configured timeout while the TCP connection is still pending. Reproduce the hanging connect phase and make timeout handling abort the request consistently without changing timeout: 0 behavior.

Higher-difficulty networking lifecycle bug involving native ClientRequest timeout semantics, abort handling, and maxRedirects transport selection.

axios-axios-low-settle-error-code

low small axios/axios

Custom validateStatus callbacks can reject statuses outside the normal 4xx and 5xx ranges, and AxiosError.code becomes undefined. Reproduce the settle behavior and ensure rejected responses always receive a defined bad-request or bad-response error code.

Focused core error-classification bug with a narrow implementation surface. Hidden tests call settle directly and require codes for custom rejected non-4xx/5xx statuses.

axios-axios-mid-fetch-global-access

mid small axios/axios

The fetch adapter can throw during module load when the internal global reference is temporarily unavailable. Reproduce the module-load failure and defer global fetch API access so adapter creation still works with explicit env overrides.

Adapter initialization bug that requires understanding module evaluation and explicit environment overrides. Hidden tests avoid browser infrastructure and check the module-load edge case directly.

fastapi-fastapi-high-pydantic-json-fast-path

high large fastapi/fastapi

FastAPI responses with a Pydantic return type or response model still go through the slower Python json.dumps path when the default response class is used. Reproduce response serialization for Pydantic models and use the direct Pydantic JSON bytes path only when no explicit response class overrides it.

Higher-difficulty routing/serialization behavior with a performance-sensitive fast path. Hidden test verifies both the optimized default path and the explicit JSONResponse fallback.

fastapi-fastapi-low-remove-vibe-decorator

low large fastapi/fastapi

A temporary April Fools API decorator was left on FastAPI instances. Remove that public decorator and its dedicated behavior without affecting normal HTTP and websocket route registration.

Low-difficulty public API cleanup. Hidden tests assert the temporary decorator is gone while ordinary route decorators continue to work.

fastapi-fastapi-mid-jsonable-encoder-color-types

mid large fastapi/fastapi

FastAPI's JSON encoder still relies on deprecated Pydantic color imports and misses the newer pydantic-extra-types color class. Reproduce color encoding with current Pydantic v2 packages and support both legacy and new color classes without import-time failures.

Mid-difficulty compatibility bug around Pydantic v2 deprecations and optional extra color types. Hidden test exercises both import paths directly.

go-gitea-gitea-high-compare-no-common-history

high large go-gitea/gitea

Repository compare views break when two branches have no common history. Reproduce the Git comparison edge case and make the compare path handle unrelated histories gracefully instead of treating the missing merge base as an unexpected command failure.

Higher-difficulty Git compare failure where the symptom is a web compare workflow but the root cause is merge-base error semantics for unrelated branch histories.

go-gitea-gitea-low-schedule-null-payload

low large go-gitea/gitea

Scheduled Actions can panic when the stored event payload is JSON null. Reproduce the schedule payload handling edge case and make scheduled runs add the schedule field without crashing or dropping already valid payload fields.

Focused scheduled-action payload bug with a narrow helper-level failure. Hidden tests cover JSON null, empty payloads, and preserving existing payload keys when adding the schedule value.

go-gitea-gitea-mid-pr-merge-self-reference

mid large go-gitea/gitea

Pull request merge commits often mention the merged PR number in their commit message. Reproduce the issue timeline behavior and prevent a merged PR from creating a commit-reference comment to itself, while keeping normal issue and pull request references working.

Mid-difficulty issue timeline bug requiring fixture-backed model behavior: skip only the PR merge commit's reference to its own PR, without suppressing unrelated commit references.

jesseduffield-lazygit-high-branch-divergence-fast-path

high small jesseduffield/lazygit

Loading divergence from configured base branches is too slow in repositories with many local branches because each branch triggers separate Git commands. Reproduce branch behind-base calculation and use Git's ahead-behind ref formatting when available, while preserving the legacy behavior on older Git versions and clearing stale values for branches omitted from fresh output.

Higher-difficulty performance bug requiring knowledge of Git version-gated ahead/behind formatting, multiple base branches, parser robustness, and stale branch state handling.

jesseduffield-lazygit-low-github-owner-casing

low small jesseduffield/lazygit

GitHub pull request integration fails to associate a local branch with its pull request when the repository owner casing differs between the PR API response and the configured remote URL. Reproduce the mismatch and make PR lookup handle owner casing consistently without changing branch-name matching.

Small Go logic bug with a focused unit-level behavior. The original PR added a regression test and changed only GitHub owner normalization.

jesseduffield-lazygit-mid-preserve-commit-message-whitespace

mid small jesseduffield/lazygit

When a commit message is remembered and restored, whitespace in the body is not preserved correctly. Reproduce the commit-message round trip and fix the message/description handling so intentional blank lines and indentation are kept, while co-author trailers are still appended cleanly.

Multi-file TUI commit-message handling bug. Hidden tests check observable string behavior around preserved body whitespace and co-author trailer insertion without requiring the original helper decomposition.

langflow-ai-langflow-high-lfx-stream-fallback

high large langflow-ai/langflow

lfx run can crash or return empty output when stream=true because streaming model output is persisted without a session/event manager to drain chunks. Reproduce the streaming handler path and fall back to a normal ainvoke result when lfx lacks the runtime context required for token streaming.

Higher-difficulty runtime bug in streaming model handling for CLI execution. Hidden test exercises the async handler with missing event-manager context and verifies it returns plain text without persisting an unconsumed stream.

langflow-ai-langflow-low-loguru-file-routing

low large langflow-ai/langflow

Custom components that log through Loguru do not write to Langflow's configured log file. Reproduce logging with a configured file target and route Loguru messages through the Langflow logger without duplicating stale file handlers.

Focused logging integration bug. Hidden test configures a temporary log file and verifies Loguru output reaches the same sink as Langflow logs.

langflow-ai-langflow-mid-mcp-connectable-inputs

mid large langflow-ai/langflow

MCP tool inputs generated from number, boolean, and JSON schema fields render as UI-only widgets instead of connectable handles. Reproduce schema-to-input conversion and make MCP-generated number and boolean inputs connectable from Message values, and make JSON object inputs connectable from JSON values, while preserving their value coercion behavior.

Mid-difficulty schema conversion and coercion bug crossing MCP model schema, input metadata, and frontend handle semantics.

louislam-uptime-kuma-high-websocket-auth-options

high medium louislam/uptime-kuma

WebSocket monitors ignore custom headers and authentication options during the upgrade handshake. Reproduce option construction for websocket checks and support headers, basic auth, OAuth client credentials, mTLS, and handshake timeouts without breaking existing status-code behavior. Expose the option construction through a reusable websocket options helper on the monitor type so it can be tested without opening a network connection; authentication headers must take precedence over stale custom Authorization headers.

Higher-difficulty monitor behavior spanning websocket handshake options and authentication precedence. Hidden tests check options directly to avoid flaky network timing.

louislam-uptime-kuma-low-submillisecond-ping-chart

low medium louislam/uptime-kuma

Monitor graphs show gaps for successful TCP checks whose average ping rounds down to 0 ms. Reproduce the chart datapoint filtering behavior and make zero-valued ping samples render while still hiding missing ping values.

Focused frontend data filtering bug. Hidden test executes the component method against zero, null, and non-zero ping datapoints without launching a browser.

louislam-uptime-kuma-mid-uptime-cleanup-buckets

mid medium louislam/uptime-kuma

Uptime cleanup lookups can create missing historical buckets, which shifts the rolling data window and makes longer-period uptime views show too little recent data. Reproduce the missing-bucket side effect and prevent cleanup lookups from mutating minutely, hourly, or daily uptime queues.

Mid-difficulty state mutation bug in rolling uptime queues. Hidden tests avoid DB-heavy update paths and directly verify key lookup side effects.

sharkdp-bat-high-fallback-syntax

high small sharkdp/bat

Users need a way to specify a syntax-highlighting fallback that applies only when automatic syntax detection fails. Add a `--fallback-syntax` option for that fallback without changing explicit language selection or normal file-name based detection behavior.

Pilot-only high case. This is broader than a bug fix and behaves more like a missing capability, so it is useful for validating runner mechanics but may be replaced in the final benchmark by a high-difficulty bug-fix PR from another repository.

sharkdp-bat-low-zip-binary-detection

low small sharkdp/bat

ZIP archive files whose first bytes are standard ZIP signatures are being treated like text in header-only output. Reproduce the behavior and make bat classify ZIP archive content as binary, without changing the behavior for ordinary text that merely starts with similar characters.

Narrow CLI behavior bug with a small implementation surface and a simple external hidden test. The original PR also added unit and integration regressions.

sharkdp-bat-mid-control-character-wrapping

mid small sharkdp/bat

When binary content is intentionally rendered as text, lines containing control characters wrap incorrectly. Reproduce the wrapping problem with a narrow terminal width and fix the displayed-width calculation so the rendered output wraps at the expected column.

Terminal rendering bug that requires understanding how non-printable characters affect display width. The hidden test checks observable CLI wrapping behavior instead of the internal helper used by the PR.

usememos-memos-high-missing-related-users

high medium usememos/memos

Memo API conversions fail when related users have been deleted, such as reactions or comments whose creators no longer exist. Reproduce the failure and make memo listing and comment/reaction conversion tolerate missing related users without hiding valid memos from existing users.

Higher-difficulty API conversion bug spanning memo, comment, reaction, and user deletion paths. Hidden tests check observable API behavior across multiple service methods.

usememos-memos-low-omit-internal-user-settings

low medium usememos/memos

Listing a user's settings returns internal-only settings such as refresh tokens and shortcuts as ordinary general settings. Reproduce the API behavior and make list responses omit internal user-setting keys while preserving normal public settings.

Focused API data-leak/regression bug in a medium application repository. Hidden tests assert response behavior rather than the converter implementation.

usememos-memos-mid-mixed-case-user-resource-names

mid medium usememos/memos

Mixed-case usernames become unusable in user resource names after login. Reproduce the API failures for users such as "Gnammi" and fix resource-name parsing and username handling so mixed-case usernames continue to work, while all-numeric usernames remain invalid.

API resource-name compatibility bug touching validation, lookup, and current user flows. Hidden tests assert mixed-case behavior and numeric rejection.

vitejs-vite-high-hmr-patch-esm-sentinel

high large vitejs/vite

In bundled dev mode, generated HMR patch files can be served as plain script content. Reproduce the patch generation behavior and make HMR patch responses contain ESM syntax so they cannot be consumed as classic cross-origin scripts, without breaking normal update notifications.

Higher-difficulty bundled-dev HMR behavior touching dev-server internals and security semantics. Hidden tests exercise patch-memory output directly while avoiding browser e2e flakiness.

vitejs-vite-low-flatten-id-sanitized-chars

low large vitejs/vite

Optimized dependency file IDs can collide or lose information when package IDs contain characters that are later sanitized for URL paths. Reproduce the flattening edge case and make flattened IDs encode every character that is unsafe in optimized-dependency file names, including literal underscores and path-special characters such as slash, dot, greater-than, plus, hash, dollar, and asterisk, without colliding with nested IDs.

Focused utility bug in optimizer ID flattening. Hidden tests check exact collision avoidance and path-safety properties for URL-sanitized characters, without requiring one exact upstream encoding spelling.

vitejs-vite-mid-deno-workspace-root

mid large vitejs/vite

Vite does not detect a Deno workspace root when a project is nested under a directory with a deno.json workspace declaration. Reproduce workspace root discovery for nested Deno workspaces and keep existing package-manager root detection behavior intact.

Mid-difficulty root-search bug requiring filesystem fixture behavior and support for Deno workspace manifests alongside existing package manager workspace detection.
